content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- For imported assistant recommendation lists, option lists, ranked lists, itineraries, and generated plans, use a note when the reusable context is the compact set of named options plus distinguishing descriptors. Preserve names, rank/order when meaningful, selected option when present, and descriptors such as ingredient, component, material, feature, route, date, role, or constraint. Do not collapse the note to only the option the user chose.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory write model uses Direct structured path, Note path, Consolidation, and Repair; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the core write model distinguishes two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths: `Direct structured path` is for safely normalized facts, stable preferences, commitments/tasks, and status changes; `Note path` is for important but not-yet-hard-fact material such as design direction, rationale, evolving plans, lessons, document digests, and contextual framing; `Consolidation` promotes mature notes when signal is strong enough; `Repair` fixes memory degradation such as duplicates, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory architecture uses four axes; In `v3`, memory is modeled along four axes: **Representation** (`Source`, `Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`), **Lifecycle** (write, reconcile, consolidate, repair, retain/archive/delete), **Retrieval policy** (`claim-first`, `note-first`, `mixed`, `evidence-first`, bounded budgets), and **Scope and lineage** (`scope_json`, `scope_text`, source refs, origin note, memory runs). These axes are meant to prevent memory from degrading into either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended v3 memory architecture pipeline; The recommended architecture routes `User / Tool / Document` input through `Source Log`, `Memory Router`, `Write-Time Retrieval`, and `Entity Canonicalizer`; then specialized writers (`Claim Extractor`, `Task Updater`, `Profile Updater`, `Note Constructor`) reconcile into `Postgres + pgvector`, which is maintained by `Background Consolidator`, `Memory Repair Worker`, and `Retention / Forgetting Worker`. At read time, `Assistant Runtime` uses `Read-Time Retrieval Planner` to query storage and feed `Answer Composer`, which returns to the runtime.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_constraint; Note path is for items that are already important but should not yet become hard facts, including design direction, rationale or trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons or patterns, document digests, and contextual framing.; scope=Note path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> primary_storage_backend; The recommended memory architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as the primary storage backend.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 4 recommended architecture
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы'
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> generated_artifact_detail; Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile entries, or episodes when the signal has become strong enough.; scope=Consolidation in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must avoid turning every turn into permanent knowledge.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы'
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> generated_artifact_detail; Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=Repair in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> generated_artifact_detail; The memory system has Note path as a semantic write path.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 semantic write paths
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must survive across single-session boundaries.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы'
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must be able to explain where a memory came from.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы'
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_constraint; Direct structured path is for items that can already be safely normalized, including explicit facts, stable preferences, commitments or tasks, and clear status changes.; scope=Direct structured path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_architecture_axis; The v3 memory architecture has Scope and lineage as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 'Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> current_metric_value; The memory system currently has two semantic paths and two maintenance paths.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы'
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> has_architecture_axis; The v3 memory architecture has Retrieval policy as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 'Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<14> generated_artifact_detail; The memory system has Direct structured path as a semantic write path.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 semantic write paths
- claim hot-path:claim:<15> has_architecture_axis; The v3 memory architecture has Lifecycle as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 'Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<16> has_architecture_axis; The v3 memory architecture has Representation as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 'Центральная архитектурная идея'

        - mention=`v3`; action=ADD_ALIAS; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=v3; reason=In this architecture document, v3 refers to the existing v3 memory architecture concept.
- mention=Structured scope; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Structured scope; reason=The section discusses structured scope as a reusable architectural concept; no existing candidate safely matches it exactly.

- mention=Tier C. Rich note memory; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=Rich note memory; reason=This is a named storage tier in the architecture and may be referenced by later claims.
- mention=Tier D. Raw evidence; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Raw evidence; reason=This is a named storage tier in the architecture and may be referenced by later claims.
- mention=Tier E. Retrieval indexes; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=CONCEPT; name=Retrieval indexes; reason=This is a named storage tier in the architecture and may be referenced by later claims.
- mention=scope_text; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<7>; type=CONCEPT; name=scope_text; reason=The document defines scope_text as a reusable structured-scope field.
- mention=scope_json; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<8>; type=CONCEPT; name=scope_json; reason=The document defines scope_json as a reusable structured-scope field.
- mention=Postgres; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<9>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Postgres; reason=Postgres is mentioned as an underlying storage technology; no candidate entity exists.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<10>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

Storage tiers ### Tier A. Core / in-context memory Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt. Что туда класть: - краткий user profile - active tasks - очень короткий session summary - system-level behavior preferences Что туда не класть: - все claims подряд - все notes подряд - raw transcript - длинные списки фактов ### Tier B. Structured long-term memory Основное typed memory-хранилище. Типы: - `Entity` - `Claim` - `Profile` - `Task` - `Episode` - `Source` - `MemoryRun` ### Tier C. Rich note memory Промежуточный semantic layer: - `Note` - `NoteSource` - `NoteEntity` - `NoteLink` Нужен для: - design direction - rationale - local conclusions - plan fragments - document digests - lessons ### Tier D. Raw evidence Immutable или почти immutable слой: - chat turns - tool outputs - imported records ### Tier E. Retrieval indexes Даже если физически все сидит в одном Postgres: - vector indexes на `sources`, `notes`, `claims`, `episodes` - full-text indexes на `content_text`, `summary`, `normalized_text` - filters по `namespace`, `entity`, `status`, `retrieval_state`, `time`, `type` ## 6. Structured scope `v2` уже хорошо чувствовал важность scope, но `v3` делает это явным. Нужно хранить одновременно: - `scope_text` - человеческое объяснение границ - `scope_json` - машинно-обрабатываемый envelope Минимум в `scope_json`: - `scope_kind` - `project_id` - `environment` - `subject_entity_id` - `conversation_id` - `modality` - `audience` Почему это важно: - reconciliation становится предсказуемее - retrieval меньше переобобщает - notes не конфликтуют ложно только потому, что обе “про базу”
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:f5dcd6c9392e5433046cb73719dbe2d9] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; title=01. Architecture; heading=01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope
Document lines: 127-208

## 5. Storage tiers

### Tier A. Core / in-context memory
Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt.

Что туда класть:
- краткий user profile
- active tasks
- очень короткий session summary
- system-level behavior preferences

Что туда не класть:
- все claims подряд
- все notes подряд
- raw transcript
- длинные списки фактов

### Tier B. Structured long-term memory
Основное typed memory-хранилище.

Типы:
- `Entity`
- `Claim`
- `Profile`
- `Task`
- `Episode`
- `Source`
- `MemoryRun`

### Tier C. Rich note memory
Промежуточный semantic layer:

- `Note`
- `NoteSource`
- `NoteEntity`
- `NoteLink`

Нужен для:
- design direction
- rationale
- local conclusions
- plan fragments
- document digests
- lessons

### Tier D. Raw evidence
Immutable или почти immutable слой:
- chat turns
- tool outputs
- imported records

### Tier E. Retrieval indexes
Даже если физически все сидит в одном Postgres:
- vector indexes на `sources`, `notes`, `claims`, `episodes`
- full-text indexes на `content_text`, `summary`, `normalized_text`
- filters по `namespace`, `entity`, `status`, `retrieval_state`, `time`, `type`

## 6. Structured scope

`v2` уже хорошо чувствовал важность scope, но `v3` делает это явным.

Нужно хранить одновременно:

- `scope_text`
  - человеческое объяснение границ
- `scope_json`
  - машинно-обрабатываемый envelope

Минимум в `scope_json`:
- `scope_kind`
- `project_id`
- `environment`
- `subject_entity_id`
- `conversation_id`
- `modality`
- `audience`

Почему это важно:
- reconciliation становится предсказуемее
- retrieval меньше переобобщает
- notes не конфликтуют ложно только потому, что обе “про базу”

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

Что туда класть: - краткий user profile - active tasks - очень короткий session summary - system-level behavior preferences Что туда не класть: - все claims подряд - все notes подряд - raw transcript - длинные списки фактов ### Tier B. Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope
Document lines: 127-208

## 5. Storage tiers

### Tier A. Core / in-context memory
Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt.

Что туда класть:
- краткий user profile
- active tasks
- очень короткий session summary
- system-level behavior preferences

Что туда не класть:
- все claims подряд
- все notes подряд
- raw transcript
- длинные списки фактов

### Tier B. Structured long-term memory
Основное typed memory-хранилище.

Типы:
- `Entity`
- `Claim`
- `Profile`
- `Task`
- `Episode`
- `Source`
- `MemoryRun`

### Tier C. Rich note memory
Промежуточный semantic layer:

- `Note`
- `NoteSource`
- `NoteEntity`
- `NoteLink`

Нужен для:
- design direction
- rationale
- local conclusions
- plan fragments
- document digests
- lessons

### Tier D. Raw evidence
Immutable или почти immutable слой:
- chat turns
- tool outputs
- imported records

### Tier E. Retrieval indexes
Даже если физически все сидит в одном Postgres:
- vector indexes на `sources`, `notes`, `claims`, `episodes`
- full-text indexes на `content_text`, `summary`, `normalized_text`
- filters по `namespace`, `entity`, `status`, `retrieval_state`, `time`, `type`

## 6. Structured scope

`v2` уже хорошо чувствовал важность scope, но `v3` делает это явным.

Нужно хранить одновременно:

- `scope_text`
  - человеческое объяснение границ
- `scope_json`
  - машинно-обрабатываемый envelope

Минимум в `scope_json`:
- `scope_kind`
- `project_id`
- `environment`
- `subject_entity_id`
- `conversation_id`
- `modality`
- `audience`

Почему это важно:
- reconciliation становится предсказуемее
- retrieval меньше переобобщает
- notes не конфликтуют ложно только потому, что обе “про базу”
